symptoms.symptom_name: Severe, rapidly worsening otalgia refractory to maximal paracetamol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe canal pain is typical; the tightly-bound canal skin makes otitis externa disproportionately painful.
Purulent canal discharge indicates active bacterial external-canal infection.
Discharge can also occur with a perforated middle-ear infection, so it is not discriminating on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain on pinna traction and tragal pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tragal/pinna-traction tenderness is the hallmark that localises the problem to the external canal.
Middle-ear infection is not typically aggravated by moving the outer ear.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blocked ear with conductive hearing loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Canal oedema and debris obstruct sound transmission, producing conductive hearing loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ear-canal itch prodrome progressing to fullness then pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pruritus preceding pain reflects the early canal-skin inflammation typical of otitis externa.
Absence of systemic toxicity lowers the probability of invasive/necrotising disease.
Necrotising otitis externa occurs almost exclusively in diabetics/immunocompromised; its absence makes the mimic unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erythematous, oedematous external auditory canal restricting view of the tympanic membrane (otoscopy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse canal erythema and oedema on otoscopy confirms external otitis — a clinician-only finding, sealed and not obtainable in telehealth.
Regional lymphadenopathy reflects local infection; requires palpation, so it is a sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of granulation tissue on the visible canal floor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Granulation at the bony-cartilaginous junction is a hallmark of necrotising disease; its absence on otoscopy reassures — clinician-only, sealed.
Near-normal temperature is consistent with a localised infection without systemic spread; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth but unverified.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Purulent otorrhoea plus pinna-traction/tragal tenderness plus an itch prodrome together localise the infection to the external canal and strongly support otitis externa.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ear canal swab for bacterial (and fungal) microscopy, culture and sensitivity
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Indicated only if the infection fails to improve after 48 hours of appropriate topical therapy, or if fungal/resistant infection is suspected — not needed to start first-line treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT/MRI of the temporal bones and skull base
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reserved for suspected necrotising otitis externa (diabetic/immunocompromised, cranial-nerve signs, granulation); over-imaging an uncomplicated case is an error.
